mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand and construction sector activity, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which improves near-term corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary overheating phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the mechanism fails during supply-driven inflation episodes where nominal earnings rise faster than prices.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when wage growth outpaces productivity, or when the unemployment rate falls due to labor force withdrawal rather than job creation.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strengthening aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the signal exhibits zero predictive power during financial crisis episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates, calculated as the federal funds rate minus core inflation, increase the cost of capital and discount future cash flows more aggressively, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive when real rates rise above 2.5%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.
